description: Page renders a partial that does not exist on disk → MissingPartial error

errors.hint: Create missing file: `app/views/partials/does/not/exist/at/all.liquid`. Use `scaffold` tool or create manually with appropriate `{% doc %}` block.
errors.hover_docs:
### render
```liquid
{% render 'filename' %}
```

---

Renders a [partial](https://documentation.platformos.com/developer-guide/platformos-workflow/directory-structure#views-pages-layouts-and-partials).

---

Inside partial, you can't directly access variables that are [created](https://documentation.platformos.com/docs/api/liquid/tags/variable-tags) outside
of the partial. However, you can specify variables as parameters
to pass outside variables to partials.

While you can't directly access created variables, you can access global objects like `context`.

Outside a partial, you can't access variables created inside it.

> Note:
> When you render a partial using the `render` tag, you can't use the [`include` tag](https://documentation.platformos.com/docs/api/liquid/tags/include)
> inside the partial.

domain_guide.rule: Pages are controllers — logic only, no inline HTML. Use graphql/render/function/redirect_to tags.

triggered_gotchas.message: Page title: use `metadata: { title: 'Static Title' }` in front matter for static titles, or `{% content_for 'title' %}Dynamic Title{% endcontent_for %}` for data-dependent titles.

Slugs use `:param` for dynamic segments (NOT `[param]`, `{param}`, or `<param>`). No leading `/`. For the home page (root `/`), omit the slug entirely — `app/views/pages/index.html.liquid` serves `/` by default. Examples: `products/:id`, `users/:user_id/orders/:id`, `search(/:query)`.
